examples.title: Binary Tree Inorder Traversal
examples.description: Recursive implementation of inorder traversal (Left-Root-Right) for binary tree.
examples.explanation: Inorder traversal visits nodes in left-root-right order. For BSTs, this produces sorted output. Base case checks for null to terminate recursion. Each node is visited exactly once.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) where h is height of tree (O(log n) balanced, O(n) skewed)

examples.title: BST Search Operation
examples.description: Search for a key in Binary Search Tree - returns node if found, NULL otherwise.
examples.code:
PROCEDURE BSTSearch(node, key)
 // Base case: node is NULL or key found
 IF node = NULL OR node.key = key THEN
 RETURN node
 END IF
 
 // Key is smaller, search left subtree
 IF key < node.key THEN
 RETURN BSTSearch(node.left, key)
 // Key is larger, search right subtree
 ELSE
 RETURN BSTSearch(node.right, key)
 END IF
END PROCEDURE

// Iterative version
PROCEDURE BSTSearchIterative(root, key)
 current = root
 WHILE current ≠ NULL AND current.key ≠ key DO
 IF key < current.key THEN
 current = current.left
 ELSE
 current = current.right
 END IF
 END WHILE
 RETURN current
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: BST search compares key with current node and decides whether to go left or right. Each comparison eliminates half the subtree (in balanced case). Recursive version uses call stack; iterative is more memory efficient.
examples.timeComplexity: O(h) = O(log n) average, O(n) worst case (skewed tree)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) for recursive, O(1) for iterative

examples.title: Level Order Traversal using Queue
examples.description: Breadth-First Search (BFS) traversal of binary tree using queue data structure.
examples.code:
PROCEDURE LevelOrderTraversal(root)
 IF root = NULL THEN
 RETURN
 END IF
 
 // Create empty queue
 queue = CreateQueue()
 Enqueue(queue, root)
 
 WHILE queue ≠ Empty DO
 // Dequeue front node
 node = Dequeue(queue)
 
 // Process current node
 PRINT node.key
 
 // Enqueue left child
 IF node.left ≠ NULL THEN
 Enqueue(queue, node.left)
 END IF
 
 // Enqueue right child
 IF node.right ≠ NULL THEN
 Enqueue(queue, node.right)
 END IF
 END WHILE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Level order uses queue (FIFO) to process nodes level by level. Root is enqueued first, then its children, ensuring children are processed after parent. This is BFS for trees. Can be used to find minimum depth, check if tree is complete, etc.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - each node enqueued and dequeued exactly once
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w) where w = maximum width of tree, O(n) in worst case (complete binary tree)
